gap_fix.recommended_kid_scene: Playground or art table: “What were you doing when I called?” “I was drawing ice cream.”
sample_english: What were you doing when I came?
I was playing on the slide.
Maya was drawing a castle.
We were looking for the ball.

coverage_rows.source_concept: Present perfect / not yet / has come

new_curriculum_chapters.title: Family, car drive, and weekend fun

coverage_rows.source_concept: Past perfect / had been / had gone

gap_fix.recommended_kid_scene: Theme park/zoo/grandma trip: “Have you been there before?” / “I had been there once.” Keep this later and optional.
sample_english: Have you been to Disneyland before?
Yes, I had been there once.
Had you gone on the big ride?
No, I had not gone on that ride.

coverage_rows.source_concept: Obligation/permission: should, must, have to, may

new_curriculum_chapters.title: How are you, and what do you want to do?
My toys, books, and game board
At the park: slides, swings, and games
Bus ride, car drive, and going places

gap_fix.recommended_kid_scene: Homework/play reward: “If we finish homework, we can play Snake and Ladder.”
sample_english: If you finish homework, we can play.
If it rains, we will play inside.
If you told me yesterday, I would have brought the game.

new_curriculum_chapters.title: New friend, ice cream, and names

Family, car drive, and weekend fun
My toys, books, and game board

gap_fix.recommended_kid_scene: Classroom/show-and-tell: “The girl who sang is my friend.” “This is the shop where we bought ice cream.”
sample_english: The boy who won the game is my friend.
This is the place where we ate ice cream.
The book that I read was funny.

gap_fix.recommended_kid_scene: Games/food/toys: bigger scoop, smaller car, faster runner, nobody plays like her.
sample_english: My scoop is bigger than yours.
This car is faster than that car.
Nobody plays Snake and Ladder like Sara.

gap_fix.recommended_kid_scene: Food/art/festival: “This cake was made by mom.” “This drawing was made by Tara.”
sample_english: This pasta was made by dad.
This card was made by Meena.
The laddus were made by grandma.

recommended_changes.source_concept: Past perfect / had been / had gone

Yes, I had been there once.
Had you gone on the big ride?
No, I had not gone on that ride.

summary_note: The new proposal covers most old source topics and common grammar, but the advanced grammar from later PDF chapters still needs deliberate kid-friendly scenes before we can say coverage is complete.
